notes: Phase order reflects the recipe's variant chain — variant 1 (clean Ctrl-D exit) → variant 2 (SIGKILL mid-idle) → variant 3 (SIGKILL mid-turn). Each variant's process_exited anchors the next variant's pre-session birth. Each variant has an explicit turn_start phase between session_birth and turn_done so the matcher finds the post-turn ready (not the session-handoff ready). Spec assertions: v1 'State is ready at exit'; v2 'Session disappears + state never entered working post-exit'; v3 'State does not stick in working'. gemini-cli has no explicit end-of-turn marker; turn_done is inferred (assistant text + zero open toolCalls).

text: Variant 1: first user prompt drives ready → working
Variant 1: turn completes back to ready (inferred turn_done — assistant text, zero open toolCalls)
Variant 1: clean Ctrl-D exit — process_exited fires AFTER state returned to ready (the v1_turn_done → v1_exit chain proves 'State is ready at exit')
Variant 2: new session appears in ready after variant 1 finishes
Variant 2: user prompt drives ready → working
Variant 2: turn completes back to ready (idle window begins)

Variant 2: SIGKILL mid-idle — process_exited fires while session is in ready; no transition to working or waiting between v2_turn_done and process_exited (satisfies 'state never entered working post-exit')
Variant 3: new session appears in ready after variant 2 finishes
Variant 3: user prompt drives ready → working
Variant 3: SIGKILL mid-turn — process_exited fires while session is in working (the recording captures the mid-turn exit; live sweep-to-ready may not appear in the 8 s tail per the recipe's notes)
